title: Subitems fields duplicate problem

posts.author: fatiksahin (Fatih Şahin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

asdasdasdasd1048×352 21.3 KB

I created two item and subitems, when i want to change same column name it can be seen as a “new” in that image, both column names change at the same time, how do i fix the name differently?

May anyone help for that issue?

Thanks in advance.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
When sub-items are added to a board, a secondary board is created behind the scenes. The rules for this secondary board are similar to those of a “primary” board. In regard to your question, this means that all of the sub-item columns (and the associated names) will always be the same for all the sub-items on that board.

If you can give more details about your use case and what it is that you want to accomplish, maybe we can help find a different way to get you what you need.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
fatiksahin:

I created two item and subitems, when i want to change same column name it can be seen as a “new” in that image, both column names change at the same time, how do i fix the name differently?

Hey @fatiksahin, could you elaborate a bit more on what you’re looking to achieve with different columns in subitems? How would it improve your workflow concretely?

I’d love to learn more to see if I can offer alternative solutions to you because what @JCorrell said is correct

posts.author: Helen1988 (Helen Warburton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I have exactly the same issue.

Our company launches new collections of fabrics. Behind the launch are 100s of tasks which need to be broken down. This involves all departments within the business so used Monday.com to help us with these actions.
I have built a full timeline of actions so far and was breaking these down in to groups thinking I could change the subitems column names at group level without changing each subitem. This didn’t work.

Would anyone be able to suggest a different way of doing this, ideally all needs to be on one board, all tracking different things.
These 2 screenshots are 2 sections of the timeline with the ideal headers.
